title: midnight-craving vietnamese nectarines bowl
description: a malty and crumbly vietnamese dish featuring dehydrated nectarines, perfect for a midnight craving. this recipe is flexitarian-friendly and a unique twist on traditional dessert recipes.
instructions: preheat the oven to 200 70f.f. on a low heat setting.
dehydrate the nectarines in the oven for 8-10 hours or overnight, checking on them periodically.
in a medium-sized bowl, combine the palm sugar, coconut flakes, crushed rice cereal, and sesame seeds.
in a separate bowl, combine the unsweetened coconut milk, vanilla extract, ginger powder, and salt.
add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients and mix until a crumbly dough forms.
add the unsalted butter and unsweetened condensed milk to the dough and mix until a cohesive mixture is formed.
if using salted egg yolks, mix them in at this stage.
using your hands, shape the dough into small balls and flatten them slightly.
place the dehydrated nectarines on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and arrange the dough balls on top of them.
bake the mixture in the oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown.
remove the mixture from the oven and let it cool on a wire rack for 10-15 minutes before serving.
